steps.title: Find angle T in radians
steps.content:
Angle $T$ is $\frac{5\pi}{12}$ radians greater than angle $R = \frac{2\pi}{3}$, so:
$$T = \frac{2\pi}{3} + \frac{5\pi}{12}$$To add these fractions, convert $\frac{2\pi}{3}$ to a denominator of 12:
$$\frac{2\pi}{3} = \frac{8\pi}{12}$$Therefore:
$$T = \frac{8\pi}{12} + \frac{5\pi}{12} = \frac{13\pi}{12}$$

steps.title: Convert from radians to degrees
steps.content:
Multiply by the conversion factor $\frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi}$:
$$T = \frac{13\pi}{12} \cdot \frac{180^{\circ}}{\pi} = \frac{13 \cdot 180^{\circ}}{12} = 13 \cdot 15^{\circ} = 195^{\circ}$$That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
